codeReview.summary: The worker correctly introduced an injectable pagination page-size seam in ProjectionPipelineService and added substantial boundary tests for conversation/search-document traversal, but the feature requirement was not fully met because artifact pagination still lacks explicit multi-page boundary coverage.

issues.description: Expected behavior requires explicit multi-page boundary coverage for search-document and artifact pagination paths. New tests added in the 3015+ block cover conversations/search documents/re-embed but do not exercise artifact pagination across multiple pages.

Existing artifact rebuild coverage (`test_rebuildJob_enqueuesReprojectAndPurgeCandidates`) validates enqueue semantics with a tiny fixture only; it does not verify deterministic offset traversal/no-skip/no-duplicate behavior across artifact page boundaries.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true`, but transcript evidence does not show a read of `.factory/library/user-testing.md`, which is a required startup read in both mission AGENTS hard-gate guidance and the indexing-efficiency-worker procedure.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for session cf39e996-5947-4259-bbcd-00cc6b9f1eda shows reads of mission.md, AGENTS.md, features.json, services.yaml, and architecture.md, but no user-testing.md read; handoff sets `followedProcedure: true` with no deviations.

summary: Review failed: pagination seam and conversation/search-document boundary tests were added successfully, but explicit multi-page artifact pagination boundary coverage is still missing relative to feature expectations.
